module

prelude
public import Lean.Meta.Reduce
public import Lean.Elab.Eval
public import Lean.Elab.Command
public import Lean.Elab.Open
import Init.Data.Nat.Order
import Init.Data.Order.Lemmas
import Init.System.Platform

public section

namespace Lean.Elab.Command

@[builtin_command_elab moduleDoc] def elabModuleDoc : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let some range ← Elab.getDeclarationRange? stx
    | return  -- must be from partial syntax, ignore

  match stx[1] with
  | Syntax.atom _ val =>
    if getMainVersoModuleDocs (← getEnv) |>.isEmpty then
      let doc := String.Pos.Raw.extract val 0 (val.rawEndPos.unoffsetBy ⟨2⟩)
      modifyEnv fun env => addMainModuleDoc env ⟨doc, range⟩
    else
      throwError m!"Can't add Markdown-format module docs because there is already Verso-format content present."
  | Syntax.node _ ``Lean.Parser.Command.versoCommentBody args =>
    let docSyntax := args.getD 0 .missing
    if docSyntax.getKind == `Lean.Doc.Syntax.parseFailure then
      -- Report parser errors without attempting elaboration
      runTermElabM fun _ => reportVersoParseFailure docSyntax
    else
      runTermElabM fun _ => do
        addVersoModDocString range ⟨docSyntax⟩
  | _ => throwErrorAt stx "unexpected module doc string{indentD <| stx}"

private def addScope (isNewNamespace : Bool) (header : String) (newNamespace : Name)
    (isNoncomputable isPublic isMeta : Bool := false) (attrs : List (TSyntax ``Parser.Term.attrInstance) := []) :
    CommandElabM Unit := do
  modify fun s => { s with
    env    := s.env.registerNamespace newNamespace,
    scopes := { s.scopes.head! with
      header := header, currNamespace := newNamespace
      isNoncomputable := s.scopes.head!.isNoncomputable || isNoncomputable
      isPublic := s.scopes.head!.isPublic || isPublic
      isMeta := s.scopes.head!.isMeta || isMeta
      attrs := s.scopes.head!.attrs ++ attrs
    } :: s.scopes
  }
  pushScope
  if isNewNamespace then
    activateScoped newNamespace

private def addScopes (header : Name) (isNewNamespace : Bool) (isNoncomputable isPublic isMeta : Bool := false)
    (attrs : List (TSyntax ``Parser.Term.attrInstance) := []) : CommandElabM Unit :=
  go header
where go
  | .anonymous => pure ()
  | .str p header => do
    go p
    let currNamespace ← getCurrNamespace
    addScope isNewNamespace header (if isNewNamespace then Name.mkStr currNamespace header else currNamespace) isNoncomputable isPublic isMeta attrs
  | _ => throwError "invalid scope"

private def addNamespace (header : Name) : CommandElabM Unit :=
  addScopes (isNewNamespace := true) (isNoncomputable := false) (attrs := []) header

private def popScopes (numScopes : Nat) : CommandElabM Unit :=
  for _ in *...numScopes do
    popScope

def withNamespace {α} (ns : Name) (elabFn : CommandElabM α) : CommandElabM α := do
  addNamespace ns
  let a ← elabFn
  modify fun s => { s with scopes := s.scopes.drop ns.getNumParts }
  popScopes ns.getNumParts
  pure a

private def innermostScopeName? : List Scope → Option Name
  | { header := "", .. } :: _ => none
  | { header := h, .. }  :: _ => some <| .mkSimple h
  | _                         => some .anonymous -- should not happen

private def checkEndHeader : Name → List Scope → Option Name
  | .anonymous, _ => none
  | .str p s, { header := h, .. } :: scopes =>
    if h == s then
      (.str · s) <$> checkEndHeader p scopes
    else
      some <| .mkSimple h
  | _, _ => some .anonymous -- should not happen

@[builtin_command_elab «namespace»] def elabNamespace : CommandElab := fun stx =>
  match stx with
  | `(namespace $n) => addNamespace n.getId
  | _               => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab «section»] def elabSection : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  match stx with
  | `(Parser.Command.section| $[@[expose%$expTk]]? $[public%$publicTk]? $[noncomputable%$ncTk]? $[meta%$metaTk]? section $(header?)?) =>
    -- TODO: allow more attributes?
    let attrs ← if expTk.isSome then
      pure [← `(Parser.Term.attrInstance| expose)]
    else
      pure []
    if let some header := header? then
      addScopes (isNewNamespace := false) (isNoncomputable := ncTk.isSome) (isPublic := publicTk.isSome) (isMeta := metaTk.isSome) (attrs := attrs) header.getId
    else
      addScope (isNewNamespace := false) (isNoncomputable := ncTk.isSome) (isPublic := publicTk.isSome) (isMeta := metaTk.isSome) (attrs := attrs) "" (← getCurrNamespace)
  | _                        => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab InternalSyntax.end_local_scope] def elabEndLocalScope : CommandElab := fun _ => do
  setDelimitsLocal

/--
Produces a `Name` composed of the names of at most the innermost `n` scopes in `ss`, truncating if an
empty scope is reached (so that we do not suggest names like `Foo.«».Bar`).

If `n` is not specified, will use all scopes in `ss`.
-/
private def nameOfScopes : (ss : List Scope) → (n : Nat := ss.length) → Name
  | _, 0 => .anonymous
  | [], _ => .anonymous
  | s :: ss, n + 1 =>
    if s.header == "" then
      .anonymous
    else
    .str (nameOfScopes ss n) s.header

/--
Returns the first suffix of `base` that begins with `seg`, if one exists.

Note: this uses a naive, O(m*n) implementation for simplicity; we assume repeated partial overlap of
name components to be relatively uncommon, so that practical performance is closer to linear.
-/
private def findSuffixWithPrefix (base : Name) (seg : Name) : Option Name :=
  findSuffixMatch base seg true
where
  /--
  Helper for `findSuffixWithPrefix`. If `allowOffset` is `false`, then `seg` must be a suffix of
  `base`, not just a prefix of a suffix.
  -/
  findSuffixMatch : (base : Name) → (seg : Name) → (allowOffset : Bool) → Option Name
  | _, .anonymous, _ => some .anonymous
  | .anonymous, _, _ => none
  | .num p n, seg@(.num p' n'), allowOffset => do
    if n == n' then
      if let some nm := findSuffixMatch p p' (allowOffset := false) then
        return .num nm n
    if allowOffset then
      return .num (← findSuffixMatch p seg allowOffset) n
    else
      none
  | .str p s, seg@(.str p' s'), allowOffset => do
    if s == s' then
      if let some nm := findSuffixMatch p p' (allowOffset := false) then
        return .str nm s
    if allowOffset then
      return .str (← findSuffixMatch p seg allowOffset) s
    else
      none
  | .str p s, seg, allowOffset =>
    if allowOffset then
      return .str (← findSuffixMatch p seg allowOffset) s
    else
      none
  | .num p n, seg, allowOffset =>
    if allowOffset then
      return .num (← findSuffixMatch p seg allowOffset) n
    else
      none

private def throwNoScope : CommandElabM Unit :=
  throwError m!"Invalid `end`: There is no current scope to end"
    ++ .note m!"Scopes are introduced using `namespace` and `section`"

private def throwMissingName (name : Name) : CommandElabM Unit := do
  let hint ← liftCoreM <| MessageData.hint m!"To end the current scope `{name}`, specify its name:"
    #[← `(end $(mkIdent name))] (codeActionPrefix? := "Add scope name: ")
  throwError "Missing name after `end`: Expected the current scope name `{name}`{hint}"

/--
Produces a hint message with a suggestion to replace the name following `end` at the current ref
with the name given by `scopes` if there is only one active scope; otherwise, returns `none`.

Rationale: When there is only one active scope, only one valid `end` command is possible, so we
suggest it; if there are multiple, then it is harder to determine with confidence which the user
intended to end.
-/
private def mkSingleScopeReplacementHint? (scopes : List Scope) := do
  -- Recall that there is always an anonymous topmost scope, so `scopes.length = 2` when there is
  -- only one active scope:
  if scopes.length == 2 then
    let name := nameOfScopes scopes
    some <$> MessageData.hint m!"Use current scope name `{name}`:" #[(← `(end $(mkIdent name)))]
      (codeActionPrefix? := "Replace scope name: ")
  else
    return none

private def throwTooManyScopeComponents (header : Name) (scopes : List Scope) : CommandElabM Unit := do
  let hint ← liftCoreM do
    if let some hint ← mkSingleScopeReplacementHint? scopes then
      pure hint
    else
      let scopesName := nameOfScopes scopes
      pure <| MessageData.hint' m!"The name after `end` must be `{scopesName}` or some suffix thereof"
  throwError m!"Invalid name after `end`: `{header}` contains too many components" ++ hint

private def throwScopeNameMismatch (header : Name) (scopes : List Scope) (endSize : Nat)
    : CommandElabM Unit := do
  let correspondingScopes := nameOfScopes scopes endSize
  let allScopes := nameOfScopes scopes
  let help ← liftCoreM do
    if let some hint ← mkSingleScopeReplacementHint? scopes then
      pure hint
    else if let some suffix := findSuffixWithPrefix allScopes header then
      let hintMsg := m!"If you meant to end the outer scope(s) `{header}`, you must end all the \
        intervening scopes `{suffix}`:"
      MessageData.hint hintMsg #[← `(end $(mkIdent suffix))]
        (codeActionPrefix? := "Add intervening scopes: ")
    else if correspondingScopes != allScopes then
      pure <| .note m!"The current scopes are `{allScopes}`"
    else pure .nil
  throwError m!"Invalid name after `end`: Expected `{correspondingScopes}`, but found `{header}`" ++ help

private def throwUnnecessaryScopeName (header : Name) : CommandElabM Unit := do
  let hintMsg := m!"Delete the name `{header}` to end the current unnamed scope; outer named scopes \
    can then be closed using additional `end` command(s):"
  let hint ← liftCoreM <| MessageData.hint hintMsg #[← `(end)] (codeActionPrefix? := "Delete name: ")
  throwError m!"Unexpected name `{header}` after `end`: The current section is unnamed" ++ hint

@[builtin_command_elab «end»] def elabEnd : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let `(end $[$header? $[.%$trailingDotTk?$_]?]?) := stx
    | throwUnsupportedSyntax
  let header? := header?.map (·.getId)
  let danglingDot := trailingDotTk?.join.isSome
  let endSize : Nat := match header? with
    | none   => 1
    | some n => n.getNumParts
  let scopes ← getScopes
  let numScopes := scopes.length
  if numScopes == 1 then
    throwNoScope
  match header? with
  | none        =>
    addCompletionInfo <| .endSection stx none false <| scopes.map (·.header)
    if let some name := innermostScopeName? scopes then
      throwMissingName name
  | some header =>
    if endSize >= numScopes then
      throwTooManyScopeComponents header scopes
    else
      addCompletionInfo <| .endSection stx header danglingDot <| scopes.map (·.header)
      let scopesName := nameOfScopes scopes endSize
      if scopesName != header then
        if scopesName == .anonymous then
          throwUnnecessaryScopeName header
        else
          throwScopeNameMismatch header scopes endSize
  modify fun s => {s with scopes := s.scopes.drop endSize }
  popScopes endSize

private partial def elabChoiceAux (cmds : Array Syntax) (i : Nat) : CommandElabM Unit :=
  if h : i < cmds.size then
    catchInternalId unsupportedSyntaxExceptionId
      (elabCommand cmds[i])
      (fun _ => elabChoiceAux cmds (i+1))
  else
    throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab choice] def elabChoice : CommandElab := fun stx =>
  elabChoiceAux stx.getArgs 0

@[builtin_command_elab «universe»] def elabUniverse : CommandElab := fun n => do
  n[1].forArgsM addUnivLevel

@[builtin_command_elab «init_quot»] def elabInitQuot : CommandElab := fun _ => do
  liftCoreM <| addDecl Declaration.quotDecl

@[builtin_command_elab «docs_to_verso»] def elabDocsToVerso : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let xs := stx[1].getArgs
  for x in xs do
    if x.getKind == identKind then -- skip commas
      let declName ← liftCoreM <| realizeGlobalConstNoOverload x
      runTermElabM <| fun _ => withRef x <| makeDocStringVerso declName
      -- Add the info afterwards so the hover shows the updated docstring
      addConstInfo x declName

@[builtin_command_elab «export»] def elabExport : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let `(export $ns ($ids*)) := stx | throwUnsupportedSyntax
  let nss ← resolveNamespace ns
  let currNamespace ← getCurrNamespace
  if nss == [currNamespace] then throwError "invalid 'export', self export"
  let mut aliases := #[]
  for idStx in ids do
    let id := idStx.getId
    let declName ← resolveNameUsingNamespaces nss idStx
    if (← getInfoState).enabled then
      addConstInfo idStx declName
    aliases := aliases.push (currNamespace ++ id, declName)
  modify fun s => { s with env := aliases.foldl (init := s.env) fun env p => addAlias env p.1 p.2 }

@[builtin_command_elab «open»] def elabOpen : CommandElab
  | `(open $decl:openDecl) => do
    let openDecls ← elabOpenDecl decl
    modifyScope fun scope => { scope with openDecls := openDecls }
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

open Lean.Parser.Term

private def typelessBinder? : Syntax → Option (Array (TSyntax [`ident, `Lean.Parser.Term.hole]) × BinderInfo)
  | `(bracketedBinderF|($ids*))     => some (ids, .default)
  | `(bracketedBinderF|{$ids*})     => some (ids, .implicit)
  | `(bracketedBinderF|⦃$ids*⦄)     => some (ids, .strictImplicit)
  | `(bracketedBinderF|[$id:ident]) => some (#[id], .instImplicit)
  | _                               => none

/--  If `id` is an identifier, return true if `ids` contains `id`. -/
private def containsId (ids : Array (TSyntax [`ident, ``Parser.Term.hole])) (id : TSyntax [`ident, ``Parser.Term.hole]) : Bool :=
  id.raw.isIdent && ids.any fun id' => id'.raw.getId == id.raw.getId

/--
  Auxiliary method for processing binder annotation update commands:
  `variable (α)`, `variable {α}`, `variable ⦃α⦄`, and `variable [α]`.
  The argument `binder` is the binder of the `variable` command.
  The method returns an array containing the "residue", that is, variables that do not correspond to updates.
  Recall that a `bracketedBinder` can be of the form `(x y)`.
  ```
  variable {α β : Type}
  variable (α γ)
  ```
  The second `variable` command updates the binder annotation for `α`, and returns "residue" `γ`.
-/
private def replaceBinderAnnotation (binder : TSyntax ``Parser.Term.bracketedBinder) : CommandElabM (Array (TSyntax ``Parser.Term.bracketedBinder)) := do
  let some (binderIds, binderInfo) := typelessBinder? binder | return #[binder]
  let varDecls := (← getScope).varDecls
  let mut varDeclsNew := #[]
  let mut binderIds := binderIds
  let mut binderIdsIniSize := binderIds.size
  let mut modifiedVarDecls := false
  -- Go through declarations in reverse to respect shadowing
  for varDecl in varDecls.reverse do
    let (ids, ty?, binderInfo') ← match varDecl with
      | `(bracketedBinderF|($ids* $[: $ty?]? $(annot?)?)) =>
        if annot?.isSome then
          for binderId in binderIds do
            if containsId ids binderId then
              throwErrorAt binderId "cannot update binder annotation of variables with default values/tactics"
        pure (ids, ty?, .default)
      | `(bracketedBinderF|{$ids* $[: $ty?]?}) =>
        pure (ids, ty?, .implicit)
      | `(bracketedBinderF|⦃$ids* $[: $ty?]?⦄) =>
        pure (ids, ty?, .strictImplicit)
      | `(bracketedBinderF|[$id : $ty]) =>
        pure (#[⟨id⟩], some ty, .instImplicit)
      | _ =>
        varDeclsNew := varDeclsNew.push varDecl; continue
    if binderInfo == binderInfo' then
      -- no update, ensure we don't have redundant annotations.
      for binderId in binderIds do
        if containsId ids binderId then
          throwErrorAt binderId "redundant binder annotation update"
      varDeclsNew := varDeclsNew.push varDecl
    else if binderIds.all fun binderId => !containsId ids binderId then
      -- `binderIds` and `ids` are disjoint
      varDeclsNew := varDeclsNew.push varDecl
    else
      let mkBinder (id : TSyntax [`ident, ``Parser.Term.hole]) (binderInfo : BinderInfo) : CommandElabM (TSyntax ``Parser.Term.bracketedBinder) :=
        match binderInfo with
        | .default => `(bracketedBinderF| ($id $[: $ty?]?))
        | .implicit => `(bracketedBinderF| {$id $[: $ty?]?})
        | .strictImplicit => `(bracketedBinderF| {{$id $[: $ty?]?}})
        | .instImplicit => do
          let some ty := ty?
            | throwErrorAt binder "cannot update binder annotation of variable `{id}` to instance implicit:\n\
                variable was originally declared without an explicit type"
          `(bracketedBinderF| [$(⟨id⟩) : $ty])
      for id in ids.reverse do
        if let some idx := binderIds.findFinIdx? fun binderId => binderId.raw.isIdent && binderId.raw.getId == id.raw.getId then
          binderIds := binderIds.eraseIdx idx
          modifiedVarDecls := true
          let newBinder ← mkBinder id binderInfo
          if binderInfo.isInstImplicit then
            -- We elaborate the new binder to make sure it's valid as instance implicit
            try
              runTermElabM fun _ => Term.withSynthesize <| Term.withAutoBoundImplicit <|
                Term.elabBinder newBinder fun _ => pure ()
            catch e =>
              throwErrorAt binder m!"cannot update binder annotation of variable `{id}` to instance implicit:\n\
                {e.toMessageData}"
          varDeclsNew := varDeclsNew.push (← mkBinder id binderInfo)
        else
          varDeclsNew := varDeclsNew.push (← mkBinder id binderInfo')
  if modifiedVarDecls then
    modifyScope fun scope => { scope with varDecls := varDeclsNew.reverse }
  if binderIds.size != binderIdsIniSize then
    binderIds.mapM fun binderId =>
      match binderInfo with
        | .default => `(bracketedBinderF| ($binderId))
        | .implicit => `(bracketedBinderF| {$binderId})
        | .strictImplicit => `(bracketedBinderF| {{$binderId}})
        | .instImplicit => throwUnsupportedSyntax
  else
    return #[binder]

@[builtin_command_elab «variable»] def elabVariable : CommandElab
  | `(variable%$tk $binders*) => do
    let binders ← binders.flatMapM replaceBinderAnnotation
    -- Try to elaborate `binders` for sanity checking
    runTermElabM fun _ => Term.withSynthesize <| Term.withAutoBoundImplicit <|
      Term.elabBinders binders fun xs => do
        -- Determine the set of auto-implicits for this variable command and add an inlay hint
        -- for them. We will only actually add the auto-implicits to a type when the variables
        -- declared here are used in some other declaration, but this is nonetheless the right
        -- place to display the inlay hint.
        let _ ← Term.addAutoBoundImplicits xs (tk.getTailPos? (canonicalOnly := true))
    -- Remark: if we want to produce error messages when variables shadow existing ones, here is the place to do it.
    for binder in binders do
      let varUIds ← (← getBracketedBinderIds binder) |>.mapM (withFreshMacroScope ∘ MonadQuotation.addMacroScope)
      modifyScope fun scope => { scope with varDecls := scope.varDecls.push binder, varUIds := scope.varUIds ++ varUIds }
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

open Meta

def elabCheckCore (ignoreStuckTC : Bool) : CommandElab
  | `(#check%$tk $term) => withoutModifyingEnv <| runTermElabM fun _ => Term.withDeclName `_check do
    -- show signature for `#check id`/`#check @id`
    if let `($id:ident) := term then
      try
        for c in (← realizeGlobalConstWithInfos term) do
          addCompletionInfo <| .id term id.getId (danglingDot := false) {} none
          logInfoAt tk <| .signature c
          return
      catch _ => pure ()  -- identifier might not be a constant but constant + projection
    let e ← Term.elabTerm term none
    Term.synthesizeSyntheticMVarsNoPostponing (ignoreStuckTC := ignoreStuckTC)
    -- Users might be testing out buggy elaborators. Let's typecheck before proceeding:
    withRef tk <| Meta.check e
    let e ← Term.levelMVarToParam (← instantiateMVars e)
    if e.isSyntheticSorry then
      return
    let type ← inferType e
    logInfoAt tk m!"{e} : {type}"
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab Lean.Parser.Command.check] def elabCheck : CommandElab := elabCheckCore (ignoreStuckTC := true)

@[builtin_command_elab Lean.reduceCmd] def elabReduce : CommandElab
  | `(#reduce%$tk $term) => go tk term
  | `(#reduce%$tk (proofs := true) $term) => go tk term (skipProofs := false)
  | `(#reduce%$tk (types := true) $term) => go tk term (skipTypes := false)
  | `(#reduce%$tk (proofs := true) (types := true) $term) => go tk term (skipProofs := false) (skipTypes := false)
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax
where
  go (tk : Syntax) (term : Syntax) (skipProofs := true) (skipTypes := true) : CommandElabM Unit :=
    withoutModifyingEnv <| runTermElabM fun _ => Term.withDeclName `_reduce do
      let e ← Term.elabTerm term none
      Term.synthesizeSyntheticMVarsNoPostponing
      -- Users might be testing out buggy elaborators. Let's typecheck before proceeding:
      withRef tk <| Meta.check e
      let e ← Term.levelMVarToParam (← instantiateMVars e)
      -- TODO: add options or notation for setting the following parameters
      withTheReader Core.Context (fun ctx => { ctx with options := ctx.options.set `smartUnfolding false }) do
        let e ← withTransparency (mode := TransparencyMode.all) <| reduce e (skipProofs := skipProofs) (skipTypes := skipTypes)
        logInfoAt tk e

def hasNoErrorMessages : CommandElabM Bool := do
  return !(← get).messages.hasErrors

def failIfSucceeds (x : CommandElabM Unit) : CommandElabM Unit := do
  let resetMessages : CommandElabM MessageLog := do
    let s ← get
    let messages := s.messages;
    modify fun s => { s with messages := {} };
    pure messages
  let restoreMessages (prevMessages : MessageLog) : CommandElabM Unit := do
    modify fun s => { s with messages := prevMessages ++ s.messages.errorsToInfos }
  let prevMessages ← resetMessages
  let succeeded ← try
    x
    hasNoErrorMessages
  catch
    | ex@(Exception.error _ _) => do logException ex; pure false
    | Exception.internal id _  => do logError (← id.getName); pure false
  finally
    restoreMessages prevMessages
  if succeeded then
    throwError "unexpected success"

@[builtin_command_elab «check_failure»] def elabCheckFailure : CommandElab
  | `(#check_failure $term) => do
    failIfSucceeds <| elabCheckCore (ignoreStuckTC := false) (← `(#check $term))
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab «synth»] def elabSynth : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let term := stx[1]
  withoutModifyingEnv <| runTermElabM fun _ => Term.withDeclName `_synth_cmd do
    let inst ← Term.elabTerm term none
    Term.synthesizeSyntheticMVarsNoPostponing
    let inst ← instantiateMVars inst
    let val  ← synthInstance inst
    logInfo val
    pure ()

@[builtin_command_elab «set_option»] def elabSetOption : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let options ← Elab.elabSetOption stx[1] stx[3]
  modify fun s => { s with maxRecDepth := maxRecDepth.get options }
  modifyScope fun scope => { scope with opts := options }

open Lean.Parser.Command.InternalSyntax in
@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.«in»] def expandInCmd : Macro
  | `($cmd₁ in%$tk $cmd₂) =>
    -- Limit ref variability for incrementality; see Note [Incremental Macros]
    withRef tk `(section $cmd₁:command $endLocalScopeSyntax:command $cmd₂ end)
  | _                 => Macro.throwUnsupported

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.addDocString] def elabAddDeclDoc : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  match stx with
  | `($doc:docComment add_decl_doc $id) =>
    let declName ← liftCoreM <| realizeGlobalConstNoOverloadWithInfo id
    unless ((← getEnv).getModuleIdxFor? declName).isNone do
      throwError "invalid 'add_decl_doc', declaration is in an imported module"
    if let .none ← findDeclarationRangesCore? declName then
      -- this is only relevant for declarations added without a declaration range
      -- in particular `Quot.mk` et al which are added by `init_quot`
      addDeclarationRangesFromSyntax declName stx id
    runTermElabM fun _ => addDocString declName (mkNullNode #[]) doc
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab Lean.Parser.Command.include] def elabInclude : CommandElab
  | `(Lean.Parser.Command.include| include $ids*) => do
    let sc ← getScope
    let vars ← sc.varDecls.flatMapM getBracketedBinderIds
    let mut uids := #[]
    for id in ids do
      if let some idx := vars.findIdx? (· == id.getId) then
        uids := uids.push sc.varUIds[idx]!
      else
        throwError "invalid 'include', variable `{id}` has not been declared in the current scope"
    modifyScope fun sc => { sc with
      includedVars := sc.includedVars ++ uids.toList
      omittedVars := sc.omittedVars.filter (!uids.contains ·) }
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab Lean.Parser.Command.omit] def elabOmit : CommandElab
  | `(Lean.Parser.Command.omit| omit $omits*) => do
    -- TODO: this really shouldn't have to re-elaborate section vars... they should come
    -- pre-elaborated
    let omittedVars ← runTermElabM fun vars => do
      Term.synthesizeSyntheticMVarsNoPostponing
      -- We don't want to store messages produced when elaborating `(getVarDecls s)` because they have already been saved when we elaborated the `variable`(s) command.
      -- So, we use `Core.resetMessageLog`.
      Core.resetMessageLog
      -- resolve each omit to variable user name or type pattern
      let elaboratedOmits : Array (Sum Name Expr) ← omits.mapM fun
        | `(ident| $id:ident) => pure <| Sum.inl id.getId
        | `(Lean.Parser.Term.instBinder| [$id : $_]) => pure <| Sum.inl id.getId
        | `(Lean.Parser.Term.instBinder| [$ty]) =>
          Sum.inr <$> Term.withoutErrToSorry (Term.elabTermAndSynthesize ty none)
        | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax
      -- check that each omit is actually used in the end
      let mut omitsUsed := omits.map fun _ => false
      let mut omittedVars := #[]
      let mut revSectionFVars : Std.HashMap FVarId Name := {}
      for (uid, var) in (← read).sectionFVars do
        revSectionFVars := revSectionFVars.insert var.fvarId! uid
      for var in vars do
        let ldecl ← var.fvarId!.getDecl
        if let some idx := (← elaboratedOmits.findIdxM? fun
            | .inl id => return ldecl.userName == id
            | .inr ty => do
              let mctx ← getMCtx
              isDefEq ty ldecl.type <* setMCtx mctx) then
          if let some uid := revSectionFVars[var.fvarId!]? then
            omittedVars := omittedVars.push uid
            omitsUsed := omitsUsed.set! idx true
          else
            throwError "invalid 'omit', `{ldecl.userName}` has not been declared in the current scope"
      for o in omits, used in omitsUsed do
        unless used do
          throwError "`{o}` did not match any variables in the current scope"
      return omittedVars
    modifyScope fun sc => { sc with
      omittedVars := sc.omittedVars ++ omittedVars.toList
      includedVars := sc.includedVars.filter (!omittedVars.contains ·) }
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab version] def elabVersion : CommandElab := fun _ => do
  let mut target := System.Platform.target
  if target.isEmpty then target := "unknown"
  -- Only one should be set, but good to know if multiple are set in error.
  let platforms :=
    (if System.Platform.isWindows then [" Windows"] else [])
    ++ (if System.Platform.isOSX then [" macOS"] else [])
    ++ (if System.Platform.isEmscripten then [" Emscripten"] else [])
  logInfo m!"Lean {Lean.versionString}\nTarget: {target}{String.join platforms}"

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.exit] def elabExit : CommandElab := fun _ =>
  logWarning "using 'exit' to interrupt Lean"

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.import] def elabImport : CommandElab := fun _ =>
  throwError "invalid 'import' command, it must be used in the beginning of the file"

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.eoi] def elabEoi : CommandElab := fun _ =>
  return

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.where] def elabWhere : CommandElab := fun _ => do
  let scope ← getScope
  let mut msg : Array MessageData := #[]
  -- Noncomputable
  if scope.isNoncomputable then
    msg := msg.push <| ← `(Parser.Command.section| noncomputable section)
  -- Namespace
  if !scope.currNamespace.isAnonymous then
    msg := msg.push <| ← `(command| namespace $(mkIdent scope.currNamespace))
  -- Open namespaces
  if let some openMsg ← describeOpenDecls scope.openDecls.reverse then
    msg := msg.push openMsg
  -- Universe levels
  if !scope.levelNames.isEmpty then
    let levels := scope.levelNames.reverse.map mkIdent
    msg := msg.push <| ← `(command| universe $levels.toArray*)
  -- Variables
  if !scope.varDecls.isEmpty then
    let varDecls : Array (TSyntax `Lean.Parser.Term.bracketedBinder) := scope.varDecls.map (⟨·.raw.unsetTrailing⟩)
    msg := msg.push <| ← `(command| variable $varDecls*)
  -- Included variables
  if !scope.includedVars.isEmpty then
    msg := msg.push <| ← `(command| include $(scope.includedVars.toArray.map (mkIdent ·.eraseMacroScopes))*)
  -- Options
  if let some optionsMsg ← describeOptions scope.opts then
    msg := msg.push optionsMsg
  if msg.isEmpty then
    logInfo m!"-- In root namespace with initial scope"
  else
    logInfo <| MessageData.joinSep msg.toList "\n\n"
where
  /--
  'Delaborate' open declarations.
  Current limitations:
  - does not check whether or not successive namespaces need `_root_`
  - does not combine commands with `renaming` clauses into a single command
  -/
  describeOpenDecls (ds : List OpenDecl) : CommandElabM (Option MessageData) := do
    let mut lines : Array MessageData := #[]
    let mut simple : Array Name := #[]
    let flush (lines : Array MessageData) (simple : Array Name) : CommandElabM (Array MessageData × Array Name) := do
      if simple.isEmpty then
        return (lines, simple)
      else
        return (lines.push <| ← `(command| open $(simple.map mkIdent)*), #[])
    for d in ds do
      match d with
      | .explicit id decl =>
        (lines, simple) ← flush lines simple
        let ns := decl.getPrefix
        let «from» := Name.mkSimple decl.getString!
        lines := lines.push <| ← `(command| open $(mkIdent ns) renaming $(mkIdent «from») → $(mkIdent id))
      | .simple ns ex =>
        if ex == [] then
          simple := simple.push ns
        else
          (lines, simple) ← flush lines simple
          lines := lines.push <| ← `(command| open $(mkIdent ns) hiding $[$(ex.toArray.map mkIdent)]*)
    (lines, _) ← flush lines simple
    return if lines.isEmpty then none else MessageData.joinSep lines.toList "\n"

  describeOptions (opts : Options) : CommandElabM (Option MessageData) := do
    let mut lines : Array MessageData := #[]
    let decls ← getOptionDecls
    for (name, val) in opts do
      -- `#guard_msgs` sets this option internally, we don't want it to end up in its output
      if name == `Elab.async then
        continue
      let (isSet, isUnknown) :=
        match decls.find? name with
        | some decl => (decl.defValue != val, false)
        | none      => (true, true)
      if isSet then
        let cmd : TSyntax `command ←
          match val with
          | .ofBool true  => `(set_option $(mkIdent name) true)
          | .ofBool false => `(set_option $(mkIdent name) false)
          | .ofString str => `(set_option $(mkIdent name) $(Syntax.mkStrLit str))
          | .ofNat n      => `(set_option $(mkIdent name) $(Syntax.mkNatLit n))
          | _             => `(set_option $(mkIdent name) 0 /- unrepresentable value -/)
        if isUnknown then
          lines := lines.push m!"-- {cmd} -- unknown option"
        else
          lines := lines.push cmd
    return if lines.isEmpty then none else MessageData.joinSep lines.toList "\n"

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.withExporting] def elabWithExporting : CommandElab
  | `(Parser.Command.withExporting| #with_exporting $cmd) =>
    withExporting do
      elabCommand cmd
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.dumpAsyncEnvState] def elabDumpAsyncEnvState : CommandElab :=
  fun _ => do
    let env ← getEnv
    IO.eprintln (← env.dbgFormatAsyncState)

end Lean.Elab.Command

17	/-!
18	
19	# Smooth morphisms
20	
21	In this file we define smooth morphisms. The main definitions are:
22	
23	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth`: A morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y` is smooth if for each affine `U ⊆ Y`
24	  and `V ⊆ f ⁻¹' U`, the induced map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is smooth.
25	
26	- `AlgebraicGeometry.SmoothOfRelativeDimension`: A morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y` is smooth of
27	  relative dimension `n` if for each `x : X` there exists an affine open neighborhood `V` of `x`
28	  and an affine open neighborhood `U` of `f.base x` with `V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U` such that the induced
29	  map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is standard smooth (of relative dimension `n`).
30	
31	## Main results
32	
33	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.iff_forall_exists_isStandardSmooth`: A morphism of schemes is smooth
34	  if and only if for each `x : X` there exists an affine open neighborhood `V` of `x`
35	  and an affine open neighborhood `U` of `f.base x` with `V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U` such that the induced
36	  map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is standard smooth.
37	
38	## Notes
39	
40	This contribution was created as part of the AIM workshop "Formalizing algebraic geometry" in
41	June 2024.
42	
43	-/
44	
45	@[expose] public section
46	
47	noncomputable section
48	
49	open CategoryTheory Limits
50	
51	universe t w v u
52	
53	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
54	
55	open RingHom
56	
57	variable (n m : ℕ) {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y)
58	
59	/-- A morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y` is smooth if for each affine `U ⊆ Y` and
60	`V ⊆ f ⁻¹' U`, The induced map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is smooth. -/
61	@[mk_iff]
62	class Smooth (f : X ⟶ Y) : Prop where
63	  smooth_appLE (f) :
64	    ∀ {U : Y.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) {V : X.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen V) (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
65	      (f.appLE U V e).hom.Smooth
66	
67	alias Scheme.Hom.smooth_appLE := Smooth.smooth_appLE
68	
69	@[deprecated (since := "2026-02-09")] alias IsSmooth := Smooth
70	
71	/-- The property of scheme morphisms `Smooth` is associated with the ring
72	homomorphism property `Smooth`. -/
73	instance : HasRingHomProperty @Smooth RingHom.Smooth where
74	  isLocal_ringHomProperty := RingHom.Smooth.propertyIsLocal
75	  eq_affineLocally' := by
76	    ext X Y f
77	    rw [smooth_iff, affineLocally_iff_forall_isAffineOpen]
78	
79	/--
80	A morphism of schemes is smooth if and only if for each `x : X` there exists an affine open
81	neighborhood `V` of `x` and an affine open neighborhood `U` of `f.base x` with `V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U`
82	such that the induced map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is standard smooth.
83	-/
84	lemma Smooth.iff_forall_exists_isStandardSmooth (f : X ⟶ Y) :
85	    Smooth f ↔
86	      ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : Y.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (V : X.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen V) (_ : x ∈ V)
87	        (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U), (f.appLE U V e).hom.IsStandardSmooth := by
88	  have : HasRingHomProperty @Smooth.{u} (Locally IsStandardSmooth) := by
89	    convert (inferInstance : HasRingHomProperty @Smooth.{u} RingHom.Smooth)
90	    ext f
91	    rw [RingHom.smooth_iff_locally_isStandardSmooth]
92	  rw [HasRingHomProperty.iff_exists_appLE_locally (P := @Smooth)]
93	  · congr!
94	    simp [Subtype.exists]
95	    grind [Scheme.affineOpens]
96	  · exact isStandardSmooth_stableUnderCompositionWithLocalizationAway.left
97	  · exact isStandardSmooth_respectsIso
98	
99	lemma Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth (f : X ⟶ Y) [Smooth f] (x : X) :
100	    ∃ (U : Y.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (V : X.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen V) (_ : x ∈ V)
101	        (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U), (f.appLE U V e).hom.IsStandardSmooth :=
102	  (iff_forall_exists_isStandardSmooth f).mp ‹_› x
103	
104	/-- Being smooth is stable under composition. -/
105	instance : MorphismProperty.IsStableUnderComposition @Smooth :=
106	  HasRingHomProperty.stableUnderComposition Smooth.stableUnderComposition
107	
108	/-- The composition of smooth morphisms is smooth. -/
109	instance smooth_comp {Z : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ Z) [Smooth f] [Smooth g] :
110	    Smooth (f ≫ g) :=
111	  MorphismProperty.comp_mem _ f g ‹Smooth f› ‹Smooth g›
112	
113	instance (priority := low) [Smooth f] : Flat f where
114	  flat_appLE {_} hU {_} hV e := (f.smooth_appLE hU hV e).flat
115	
116	/-- Smooth is stable under base change. -/
117	instance smooth_isStableUnderBaseChange : MorphismProperty.IsStableUnderBaseChange @Smooth :=
118	  HasRingHomProperty.isStableUnderBaseChange Smooth.isStableUnderBaseChange
119	
120	instance : MorphismProperty.Respects @Smooth @IsOpenImmersion :=
121	  HasRingHomProperty.respects_isOpenImmersion
122	    (RingHom.Smooth.stableUnderComposition.stableUnderCompositionWithLocalizationAway
123	      RingHom.Smooth.holdsForLocalizationAway).1
124	
125	@[deprecated (since := "2026-02-09")]
126	alias isSmooth_isStableUnderBaseChange := smooth_isStableUnderBaseChange
127	
128	/--
129	A morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y` is smooth of relative dimension `n` if for each `x : X` there
130	exists an affine open neighborhood `V` of `x` and an affine open neighborhood `U` of
131	`f.base x` with `V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U` such that the induced map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is
132	standard smooth of relative dimension `n`.
133	-/
134	@[mk_iff]
135	class SmoothOfRelativeDimension : Prop where
136	  exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension : ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : Y.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen U)
137	    (V : X.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen V) (_ : x ∈ V) (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
138	    IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n (f.appLE U V e).hom
139	
140	@[deprecated (since := "2026-02-09")] alias IsSmoothOfRelativeDimension := SmoothOfRelativeDimension
141	
142	/-- If `f` is smooth of any relative dimension, it is smooth. -/
143	lemma SmoothOfRelativeDimension.smooth [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n f] : Smooth f := by
144	  rw [Smooth.iff_forall_exists_isStandardSmooth]
145	  intro x
146	  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hx, e, hf⟩ := exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension (n := n) (f := f) x
147	  exact ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hx, e, hf.isStandardSmooth⟩
148	
149	@[deprecated (since := "2026-02-09")]
150	alias IsSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isSmooth := SmoothOfRelativeDimension.smooth
151	
152	/-- The property of scheme morphisms `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` is associated with the ring
153	homomorphism property `Locally (IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n)`. -/
154	instance : HasRingHomProperty (@SmoothOfRelativeDimension n)
155	    (Locally (IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n)) := by
156	  apply HasRingHomProperty.locally_of_iff
157	  · exact (isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_localizationPreserves n).away
158	  · exact isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_stableUnderCompositionWithLocalizationAway n
159	  · intro X Y f
160	    rw [smoothOfRelativeDimension_iff]
161	    congr!
162	    simp [Subtype.exists]
163	    grind [Scheme.affineOpens]
164	
165	/-- Smooth of relative dimension `n` is stable under base change. -/
166	lemma smoothOfRelativeDimension_isStableUnderBaseChange :
167	    MorphismProperty.IsStableUnderBaseChange (@SmoothOfRelativeDimension n) :=
168	  HasRingHomProperty.isStableUnderBaseChange <| locally_isStableUnderBaseChange
169	    isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_respectsIso
170	    (isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_isStableUnderBaseChange n)
171	
172	@[deprecated (since := "2026-02-09")]
173	alias isSmoothOfRelativeDimension_isStableUnderBaseChange :=
174	  smoothOfRelativeDimension_isStableUnderBaseChange

176	/-- Open immersions are smooth of relative dimension `0`. -/
177	instance (priority := 900) [IsOpenImmersion f] : SmoothOfRelativeDimension 0 f :=
178	  HasRingHomProperty.of_isOpenImmersion
179	    (locally_holdsForLocalizationAway <|
180	      isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_holdsForLocalizationAway).containsIdentities
181	
182	/-- Open immersions are smooth. -/
183	instance (priority := 900) [IsOpenImmersion f] : Smooth f :=
184	  SmoothOfRelativeDimension.smooth 0 f

186	instance {X Y S : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ S) (g : Y ⟶ S) [Smooth g] :
187	    Smooth (pullback.fst f g) :=
188	  MorphismProperty.pullback_fst f g inferInstance
189	
190	instance {X Y S : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ S) (g : Y ⟶ S) [Smooth f] :
191	    Smooth (pullback.snd f g) :=
192	  MorphismProperty.pullback_snd f g inferInstance

194	instance (f : X ⟶ Y) (V : Y.Opens) [Smooth f] : Smooth (f ∣_ V) :=
195	  IsZariskiLocalAtTarget.restrict ‹_› V
196	
197	instance (f : X ⟶ Y) (U : X.Opens) (V : Y.Opens) (e) [Smooth f] :
198	    Smooth (f.resLE V U e) := by
199	  delta Scheme.Hom.resLE; infer_instance

201	/-- If `f` is smooth of relative dimension `n` and `g` is smooth of relative dimension
202	`m`, then `f ≫ g` is smooth of relative dimension `n + m`. -/
203	instance smoothOfRelativeDimension_comp {Z : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ Z)
204	    [hf : SmoothOfRelativeDimension n f] [hg : SmoothOfRelativeDimension m g] :
205	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension (n + m) (f ≫ g) where
206	  exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension x := by
207	    obtain ⟨U₂, hU₂, V₂, hV₂, hfx₂, e₂, hf₂⟩ := hg.exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension (f x)
208	    obtain ⟨U₁', hU₁', V₁', hV₁', hx₁', e₁', hf₁'⟩ :=
209	      hf.exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension x
210	    obtain ⟨r, s, hx₁, e₁, hf₁⟩ := exists_basicOpen_le_appLE_of_appLE_of_isAffine
211	      (isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_stableUnderCompositionWithLocalizationAway n).right
212	      (isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_localizationPreserves n).away
213	      x ⟨V₂, hV₂⟩ ⟨U₁', hU₁'⟩ ⟨V₁', hV₁'⟩ ⟨V₁', hV₁'⟩ hx₁' hx₁' e₁' hf₁' hfx₂
214	    have e : X.basicOpen s ≤ (f ≫ g) ⁻¹ᵁ U₂ :=
215	      le_trans e₁ <| f.preimage_mono <| le_trans (Y.basicOpen_le r) e₂
216	    have heq : (f ≫ g).appLE U₂ (X.basicOpen s) e = g.appLE U₂ V₂ e₂ ≫
217	        CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap Γ(Y, V₂) Γ(Y, Y.basicOpen r)) ≫
218	          f.appLE (Y.basicOpen r) (X.basicOpen s) e₁ := by
219	      rw [RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra, CommRingCat.ofHom_hom,
220	        g.appLE_map_assoc, Scheme.Hom.appLE_comp_appLE]
221	    refine ⟨U₂, hU₂, X.basicOpen s, hV₁'.basicOpen s, hx₁, e, heq ▸ ?_⟩
222	    apply IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.comp ?_ hf₂
223	    haveI : IsLocalization.Away r Γ(Y, Y.basicOpen r) := hV₂.isLocalization_basicOpen r
224	    exact (isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_stableUnderCompositionWithLocalizationAway n).left
225	      _ r _ hf₁

229	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension 0 (f ≫ g) :=
230	  inferInstanceAs <| SmoothOfRelativeDimension (0 + 0) (f ≫ g)

239	  rw [HasRingHomProperty.eq_affineLocally @LocallyOfFinitePresentation]
240	  rw [HasRingHomProperty.eq_affineLocally @Smooth] at hf
241	  exact affineLocally_le (fun hf ↦ hf.finitePresentation) f hf

243	lemma formallySmooth_stalkMap_iff {f : X ⟶ Y} {x : X} (U : Y.Opens)
244	      (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (V : X.Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (hVU : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U)
245	      (hx : x ∈ V) :
246	    letI := (f.appLE U V hVU).hom.toAlgebra
247	    (f.stalkMap x).hom.FormallySmooth ↔
248	      hV.primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩ ∈ Algebra.smoothLocus Γ(Y, U) Γ(X, V) := by
249	  letI := (f.appLE U V hVU).hom.toAlgebra
250	  have : (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩).asIdeal.LiesOver (hU.primeIdealOf ⟨f x, hVU hx⟩).asIdeal :=
251	    ⟨congr($(IsAffineOpen.comap_primeIdealOf_appLE U hU V hV hVU hx).1).symm⟩
252	  trans Algebra.FormallySmooth
253	    (Localization.AtPrime (hU.primeIdealOf ⟨f x, hVU hx⟩).asIdeal)
254	    (Localization.AtPrime (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩).asIdeal)
255	  · rw [← formallySmooth_algebraMap]
256	    exact RingHom.FormallySmooth.respectsIso.arrow_mk_iso_iff
257	      (IsAffineOpen.arrowStalkMapIso f U hU V hV hVU hx)
258	  · exact Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_restrictScalars.symm

260	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
261	lemma exists_smooth_of_formallySmooth_stalk
262	    (f : X ⟶ Y) [LocallyOfFinitePresentation f]
263	    (x : X) (H : (f.stalkMap x).hom.FormallySmooth) :
264	    ∃ (U : Y.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (V : X.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen V) (hVU : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
265	      x ∈ V ∧ (f.appLE U V hVU).hom.Smooth := by
266	  obtain ⟨_, ⟨U, hU, rfl⟩, hxU, -⟩ :=
267	    Y.isBasis_affineOpens.exists_subset_of_mem_open (Set.mem_univ (f x)) isOpen_univ
268	  obtain ⟨_, ⟨V, hV, rfl⟩, hxV, hVU⟩ :=
269	    X.isBasis_affineOpens.exists_subset_of_mem_open hxU (U.2.preimage f.continuous)
270	  have := f.finitePresentation_appLE hU hV hVU
271	  algebraize [(f.appLE U V hVU).hom]
272	  have : Algebra.IsSmoothAt _ _ := (formallySmooth_stalkMap_iff U hU V hV hVU hxV).mp H
273	  obtain ⟨r, hrx, hr⟩ := Algebra.IsSmoothAt.exists_notMem_smooth Γ(Y, U)
274	    (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨x, hxV⟩).asIdeal
275	  refine ⟨_, hU, _, hV.basicOpen r, (X.basicOpen_le r).trans hVU, ?_, ?_⟩
276	  · rwa [← PrimeSpectrum.mem_basicOpen, IsAffineOpen.primeIdealOf,
277	      ← hV.fromSpec_preimage_basicOpen, Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply,
278	      IsAffineOpen.isoSpec_hom, IsAffineOpen.toSpecΓ_fromSpec] at hrx
279	  · have := hV.isLocalization_basicOpen r
280	    rw [← RingHom.smooth_algebraMap] at hr
281	    convert RingHom.Smooth.propertyIsLocal.respectsIso.1 _
282	      (IsLocalization.algEquiv (.powers r) _ Γ(X, X.basicOpen r)).toRingEquiv hr
283	    ext
284	    dsimp
285	    simp only [IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply Γ(Y, U) Γ(X, V) (Localization _),
286	      IsLocalization.map_eq]
287	    simp only [algebraMap_toAlgebra, RingHomCompTriple.comp_apply, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
288	      Scheme.Hom.appLE_map]

290	lemma Scheme.Hom.isOpen_smoothLocus [LocallyOfFinitePresentation f] :
291	    IsOpen { x | (f.stalkMap x).hom.FormallySmooth } := by
292	  refine isOpen_iff_forall_mem_open.mpr fun x hx ↦ ?_
293	  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hVU, hxV, H⟩ := exists_smooth_of_formallySmooth_stalk f x hx
294	  algebraize [(f.appLE U V hVU).hom]
295	  exact ⟨V, fun y hy ↦ (formallySmooth_stalkMap_iff U hU V hV hVU hy).mpr
296	    (inferInstanceAs (Algebra.IsSmoothAt _ _)), V.2, hxV⟩
297	
298	/-- The set of points smooth over a base, as a `Scheme.Opens`. -/
299	def Scheme.Hom.smoothLocus (f : X ⟶ Y) [LocallyOfFinitePresentation f] : X.Opens :=
300	  ⟨{ x | (f.stalkMap x).hom.FormallySmooth }, f.isOpen_smoothLocus⟩
301	
302	lemma Scheme.Hom.mem_smoothLocus {f : X ⟶ Y} [LocallyOfFinitePresentation f] {x : X} :
303	    x ∈ f.smoothLocus ↔ (f.stalkMap x).hom.FormallySmooth := .rfl
304	
305	lemma Scheme.Hom.smoothLocus_eq_top (f : X ⟶ Y) [Smooth f] :
306	    f.smoothLocus = ⊤ := by
307	  rw [← top_le_iff]
308	  rintro x -
309	  obtain ⟨_, ⟨U, hU, rfl⟩, hxU, -⟩ :=
310	    Y.isBasis_affineOpens.exists_subset_of_mem_open (Set.mem_univ (f x)) isOpen_univ
311	  obtain ⟨_, ⟨V, hV, rfl⟩, hxV, hVU⟩ :=
312	    X.isBasis_affineOpens.exists_subset_of_mem_open hxU (U.2.preimage f.continuous)
313	  have := f.smooth_appLE hU hV hVU
314	  algebraize [(f.appLE U V hVU).hom]
315	  rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_smoothLocus, formallySmooth_stalkMap_iff U hU V hV hVU hxV]
316	  exact inferInstanceAs (Algebra.IsSmoothAt _ _)
317	
318	lemma Scheme.Hom.smoothLocus_eq_top_iff {f : X ⟶ Y} [LocallyOfFinitePresentation f] :
319	    f.smoothLocus = ⊤ ↔ Smooth f := by
320	  refine ⟨fun H ↦ ?_, fun _ ↦ f.smoothLocus_eq_top⟩
321	  refine IsZariskiLocalAtSource.iff_exists_resLE.mpr fun x ↦ ?_
322	  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hVU, hxV, H⟩ :=
323	    exists_smooth_of_formallySmooth_stalk f _ (H.ge (Set.mem_univ x))
324	  refine ⟨U, V, hxV, hVU, ?_⟩
325	  have : IsAffine _ := hU
326	  have : IsAffine _ := hV
327	  rw [HasRingHomProperty.iff_of_isAffine (P := @Smooth)]
328	  exact (RingHom.Smooth.propertyIsLocal.respectsIso.arrow_mk_iso_iff
329	    (arrowResLEAppIso f U V hVU)).mpr H

331	lemma Scheme.Hom.preimage_smoothLocus_eq {U : Scheme.{u}}
332	    (f : U ⟶ X) (g : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] [LocallyOfFinitePresentation g] :
333	    f ⁻¹ᵁ g.smoothLocus = (f ≫ g).smoothLocus := by
334	  ext x
335	  refine (RingHom.FormallySmooth.respectsIso.cancel_right_isIso _ (f.stalkMap x)).symm.trans ?_
336	  rw [← CommRingCat.hom_comp, ← stalkMap_comp]
337	  rfl

339	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
340	lemma Scheme.Hom.genericPoint_mem_smoothLocus_of_perfectField
341	    {K : Type u} [Field K] [PerfectField K] [IsIntegral X]
342	    (f : X ⟶ Spec (.of K)) [LocallyOfFinitePresentation f] : genericPoint X ∈ f.smoothLocus := by
343	  have := LocallyOfFiniteType.stalkMap f (genericPoint X)
344	  rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_smoothLocus]
345	  algebraize [(f.stalkMap (genericPoint X)).hom]
346	  let K' := (Spec.structureSheaf K).presheaf.stalk (f (genericPoint X))
347	  let e : K ≃ₐ[K] K' := IsLocalization.atUnits _ (f (genericPoint X)).asIdeal.primeCompl
348	      (fun x hx ↦ by aesop (add simp IsUnit.mem_submonoid_iff))
349	  have : Algebra.IsAlgebraic K K' :=
350	    .of_injective e.symm.toAlgHom e.symm.injective
351	  let : Field K' := (e.toRingEquiv.symm.isField (Field.toIsField K)).toField
352	  let : Field ((Spec (.of K)).presheaf.stalk (f (genericPoint X))) := this
353	  have : PerfectField ((Spec (.of K)).presheaf.stalk (f (genericPoint X))) :=
354	    Algebra.IsAlgebraic.perfectField (K := K)
355	      (L := (Spec.structureSheaf K).presheaf.stalk (f (genericPoint X)))
356	  exact Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_perfectField

358	instance {X : Scheme} [IsReduced X] (U : X.Opens) : IsReduced U :=
359	  isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion U.ι

362	lemma Scheme.Hom.dense_smoothLocus_of_perfectField
363	    {K : Type u} [Field K] [PerfectField K] [IsReduced X]
364	    (f : X ⟶ Spec (.of K)) [LocallyOfFinitePresentation f] : Dense (f.smoothLocus : Set X) := by
365	  wlog H : CompactSpace X generalizing X
366	  · rw [dense_iff_closure_eq, Set.eq_univ_iff_forall]
367	    intro x
368	    obtain ⟨_, ⟨U : X.Opens, hU, rfl⟩, hxU, -⟩ :=
369	      X.isBasis_affineOpens.exists_subset_of_mem_open (Set.mem_univ x) isOpen_univ
370	    have := this (U.ι ≫ f) (isCompact_iff_compactSpace.mp hU.isCompact) ⟨x, hxU⟩
371	    rwa [← preimage_smoothLocus_eq, Scheme.Hom.coe_preimage,
372	      ← U.ι.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.preimage_closure_eq_closure_preimage U.ι.continuous,
373	      Set.mem_preimage, U.ι_apply] at this
374	  have : IsNoetherian X := { __ := LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian f }
375	  rw [dense_iff_closure_eq, Set.eq_univ_iff_forall]
376	  intro x
377	  let U : X.Opens :=
378	    ⟨(⋃₀ (irreducibleComponents X \ {irreducibleComponent x}))ᶜ, by
379	      rw [Set.sUnion_eq_biUnion, isOpen_compl_iff]
380	      exact TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents.diff.isClosed_biUnion
381	        fun W hW ↦ isClosed_of_mem_irreducibleComponents W hW.1⟩
382	  have hU : closure U = irreducibleComponent x :=
383	    closure_sUnion_irreducibleComponents_diff_singleton
384	      TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents
385	      _ (irreducibleComponent_mem_irreducibleComponents x)
386	  have : AlgebraicGeometry.IsIntegral U :=
387	    have : IrreducibleSpace U := isIrreducible_iff_irreducibleSpace.mp
388	      (isIrreducible_iff_closure.mp (hU ▸ isIrreducible_irreducibleComponent))
389	    isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced _
390	  have : U.ι (genericPoint U) ∈ f.smoothLocus := by
391	    have := (U.ι ≫ f).genericPoint_mem_smoothLocus_of_perfectField
392	    rwa [← preimage_smoothLocus_eq, Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage] at this
393	  exact (((genericPoint_spec U).image U.ι.continuous).specializes (y := x)
394	    (by rw [Set.image_univ, U.range_ι, hU]; exact mem_irreducibleComponent)).mem_closed
395	    isClosed_closure (subset_closure this)
396

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Extension.Cotangent.Basis
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Extension.Cotangent.Free
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Locus
11	
12	/-!
13	# Standard smooth of free Kaehler differentials
14	
15	In this file we show a presentation independent characterization of being
16	standard smooth: An `R`-algebra `S` of finite presentation is standard smooth if and only if
17	`H¹(S/R) = 0` and `Ω[S⁄R]` is free on `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` for some `sᵢ : S`.
18	
19	From this we deduce relations of standard smooth with other local properties.
20	
21	## Main results
22	
23	- `IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential`: An `R`-algebra `S` of finite
24	  presentation is standard smooth if and only if `H¹(S/R) = 0` and `Ω[S⁄R]` is free on
25	  `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` for some `sᵢ : S`.
26	- `Etale.iff_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_zero`: An `R`-algebra `S` is
27	  étale if and only if it is standard smooth of relative dimension zero.
28	- `IsSmoothAt.exists_notMem_isStandardSmooth`: If `S` is `R`-smooth at a prime `p`,
29	  it is standard smooth on a standard open containing `p`.
30	
31	## Notes
32	
33	For an example of an algebra with `H¹(S/R) = 0` and `Ω[S⁄R]` finite and free, but
34	`S` not standard smooth over `R`, consider `R = ℝ` and `S = R[x,y]/(x² + y² - 1)` the
35	coordinate ring of the circle. One can show that then `Ω[S⁄R]` is `S`-free on `ω = xdy - ydx`,
36	but there are no `f g : S` such that `ω = g df`.
37	-/
38	
39	public section
40	
41	namespace Algebra
42	
43	open KaehlerDifferential
44	
45	variable {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
46	
47	/-- If `H¹(S/R) = 0` and `Ω[S⁄R]` is free on `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` for some `sᵢ : S`, then `S`
48	is `R`-standard smooth. -/
49	theorem IsStandardSmooth.of_basis_kaehlerDifferential [FinitePresentation R S]
50	    [Subsingleton (H1Cotangent R S)]
51	    {I : Type*} (b : Module.Basis I S (Ω[S⁄R])) (hb : Set.range b ⊆ Set.range (D R S)) :
52	    IsStandardSmooth R S := by
53	  nontriviality S
54	  obtain ⟨n, ⟨P⟩⟩ := (FiniteType.iff_exists_generators (R := R) (S := S)).mp inferInstance
55	  choose f' hf' using hb
56	  let P := P.extend fun i ↦ f' ⟨i, rfl⟩
57	  have hb (i : I) : b i = D R S (P.val (Sum.inr i)) := by simp [P, hf']
58	  have : Function.Bijective (P.cotangentRestrict _) :=
59	    P.cotangentRestrict_bijective_of_basis_kaehlerDifferential Sum.inl_injective
60	      Set.isCompl_range_inl_range_inr.symm b hb
61	  let bcot' : Module.Basis (Fin n) S P.toExtension.Cotangent :=
62	    .ofRepr (.ofBijective (P.cotangentRestrict _) this)
63	  have : Finite I := Module.Finite.finite_basis b
64	  obtain ⟨Q, bcot, hcomp, hbcot⟩ := P.exists_presentation_of_basis_cotangent bcot'
65	  let P' : PreSubmersivePresentation R S (Unit ⊕ Fin n ⊕ I) (Unit ⊕ Fin n) :=
66	    { __ := Q
67	      map := Sum.map _root_.id Sum.inl
68	      map_inj := Sum.map_injective.mpr ⟨fun _ _ h ↦ h, Sum.inl_injective⟩ }
69	  have hcompl : IsCompl (Set.range (Sum.inr ∘ Sum.inr)) (Set.range P'.map) := by
70	    simp [P', ← eq_compl_iff_isCompl, Set.ext_iff, Set.mem_compl_iff]
71	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (P'.cotangentRestrict P'.map_inj) := by
72	    apply P'.cotangentRestrict_bijective_of_basis_kaehlerDifferential P'.map_inj hcompl b
73	    intro k
74	    simp only [hb, ← hcomp, P', Function.comp_def]
75	  let P'' : SubmersivePresentation R S _ _ :=
76	    ⟨P', P'.isUnit_jacobian_of_cotangentRestrict_bijective bcot hbcot hbij⟩
77	  exact P''.isStandardSmooth
78	
79	/-- An `R`-algebra `S` of finite presentation is standard smooth if and only if
80	`H¹(S/R) = 0` and `Ω[S⁄R]` is free on `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` for some `sᵢ : S`. -/
81	theorem IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential [FinitePresentation R S] :
82	    IsStandardSmooth R S ↔ Subsingleton (H1Cotangent R S) ∧
83	      ∃ (I : Type) (b : Module.Basis I S (Ω[S⁄R])), Set.range b ⊆ Set.range (D R S) := by
84	  refine ⟨fun h ↦ ⟨inferInstance, ?_⟩, fun ⟨h, ⟨_, b, hb⟩⟩ ↦ .of_basis_kaehlerDifferential b hb⟩
85	  obtain ⟨ι, σ, _, _, ⟨P⟩⟩ := Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.out (R := R) (S := S)
86	  exact ⟨_, P.basisKaehler, by simp [Set.range_subset_iff]⟩
87	
88	/-- `S` is an étale `R`-algebra if and only if it is standard smooth of relative dimension `0`. -/
89	theorem Etale.iff_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_zero :
90	    Etale R S ↔ IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 0 R S := by
91	  refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, fun _ ↦ inferInstance⟩
92	  nontriviality S
93	  suffices h : IsStandardSmooth R S by
94	    simp [IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.iff_of_isStandardSmooth]
95	  rw [IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential]
96	  refine ⟨inferInstance, ⟨Empty, Module.Basis.empty Ω[S⁄R], ?_⟩⟩
97	  simp [Set.range_subset_iff]
98	
99	variable (R) in
100	/-- If `S` is `R`-smooth at a prime `p`, then `S` is `R`-standard-smooth in a neighbourhood of `p`:
101	there exists a basic open `p ∈ D(f)` of `Spec S` such that `S[1/f]` is standard smooth. -/
102	theorem IsSmoothAt.exists_notMem_isStandardSmooth [FinitePresentation R S] (p : Ideal S) [p.IsPrime]
103	    [IsSmoothAt R p] :
104	    ∃ (f : S), f ∉ p ∧ IsStandardSmooth R (Localization.Away f) := by
105	  -- By replacing `S` by some `S[1/g]` we may assume `S` is globally smooth.
106	  wlog h : Smooth R S
107	  · obtain ⟨g, hg, hsm⟩ := IsSmoothAt.exists_notMem_smooth R p
108	    have _ : (Ideal.map (algebraMap S (Localization.Away g)) p).IsPrime := by
109	      apply IsLocalization.isPrime_of_isPrime_disjoint (.powers g) _ _ ‹_›
110	      rwa [Ideal.disjoint_powers_iff_notMem _ (Ideal.IsPrime.isRadical ‹_›)]
111	    obtain ⟨g', hg', hstd⟩ := this (R := R) (p.map (algebraMap S (Localization.Away g))) hsm
112	    have : IsLocalization.Away (g * (IsLocalization.Away.sec g g').1) (Localization.Away g') :=
113	      .mul_of_associated _ _ g' <| IsLocalization.Away.associated_sec_fst g g'
114	    let e : Localization.Away (g * (IsLocalization.Away.sec g g').1) ≃ₐ[S] Localization.Away g' :=
115	      Localization.algEquiv _ _
116	    refine ⟨g * (IsLocalization.Away.sec g g').1, ?_, .of_algEquiv (e.restrictScalars R).symm⟩
117	    refine Ideal.IsPrime.mul_notMem ‹_› hg fun hmem ↦ hg' ?_
118	    rw [Ideal.mem_iff_of_associated (IsLocalization.Away.associated_sec_fst g g').symm]
119	    exact Ideal.mem_map_of_mem (algebraMap S (Localization.Away g)) hmem
120	  -- `Ω[Sₚ⁄R]` is projective, so free over the local ring `Sₚ` and
121	  -- a basis extends to a neighbourhood `D(g)`.
122	  obtain ⟨κ, a, b, hb⟩ := Module.exists_basis_of_span_of_flat _
123	    (span_range_map_derivation_of_isLocalization R _ (Localization.AtPrime p) p.primeCompl)
124	  let e : (κ →₀ S) →ₗ[S] Ω[S⁄R] :=
125	    Finsupp.linearCombination S fun i : κ ↦ D R S (a i)
126	  let l₁ : (κ →₀ S) →ₗ[S] (κ →₀ Localization.AtPrime p) :=
127	    Finsupp.mapRange.linearMap (Algebra.linearMap S (Localization.AtPrime p))
128	  let l₂ : Ω[S⁄R] →ₗ[S] Ω[Localization.AtPrime p⁄R] := map R R S (Localization.AtPrime p)
129	  let eₚ : (κ →₀ Localization.AtPrime p) →ₗ[Localization.AtPrime p] Ω[Localization.AtPrime p⁄R] :=
130	    IsLocalizedModule.mapExtendScalars p.primeCompl l₁ l₂ (Localization.AtPrime p) e
131	  have : eₚ = b.repr.symm := by
132	    ext i
133	    trans IsLocalizedModule.map p.primeCompl l₁ l₂ e <| l₁ <| Finsupp.single i 1
134	    · simp [eₚ, -IsLocalizedModule.map_apply, l₁]
135	    · simp [l₂, e, hb]
136	  have heₚ : Function.Bijective eₚ := this ▸ b.repr.symm.bijective
137	  have : Finite κ := Module.Finite.finite_basis b
138	  obtain ⟨g, hg, h⟩ := Module.FinitePresentation.exists_notMem_bijective e p l₁ l₂ heₚ
139	  let l₁ₜ : (κ →₀ S) →ₗ[S] (κ →₀ Localization.Away g) :=
140	    Finsupp.mapRange.linearMap (Algebra.linearMap S _)
141	  let l₂ₜ : Ω[S⁄R] →ₗ[S] Ω[Localization.Away g⁄R] :=
142	    map R R S (Localization.Away g)
143	  rw [← IsLocalizedModule.map_bijective_iff_localizedModuleMap_bijective l₁ₜ l₂ₜ] at h
144	  let eₜ' : (κ →₀ Localization.Away g) →ₗ[Localization.Away g] Ω[Localization.Away g⁄R] :=
145	    IsLocalizedModule.mapExtendScalars (Submonoid.powers g) l₁ₜ l₂ₜ (Localization.Away g) e
146	  refine ⟨g, hg, .of_basis_kaehlerDifferential (.ofRepr (LinearEquiv.ofBijective eₜ' h).symm) ?_⟩
147	  rintro - ⟨i, rfl⟩
148	  exact ⟨algebraMap S _ (a i), by simp +zetaDelta [IsLocalizedModule.map_linearCombination]⟩
149	
150	variable (R S) in
151	/-- If `S` is `R`-smooth, there exists a cover by basic opens `D(sᵢ)` such that
152	`S[1/sᵢ]` is `R`-standard-smooth. -/
153	theorem Smooth.exists_span_eq_top_isStandardSmooth [Smooth R S] :
154	    ∃ (s : Set S), Ideal.span s = ⊤ ∧ ∀ x ∈ s, IsStandardSmooth R (Localization.Away x) := by
155	  choose f hf₁ hf₂ using IsSmoothAt.exists_notMem_isStandardSmooth R (S := S)
156	  /- #adaptation_note Before https://github.com/leanprover/lean4/pull/13166
157	  (replacing grind's canonicalizer with a type-directed normalizer), the original proof was:
158	  `refine ⟨Set.range (fun p : PrimeSpectrum S ↦ f p.asIdeal), ?_, by grind⟩`
159	  It is not yet clear whether this is due to defeq abuse in Mathlib or a problem in the new
160	  canonicalizer; a minimization would help. -/
161	  refine ⟨Set.range (fun p : PrimeSpectrum S ↦ f p.asIdeal), ?_, by
162	    simp only [Set.mem_range, forall_exists_index, forall_apply_eq_imp_iff]; intro; apply hf₂⟩
163	  simp [← PrimeSpectrum.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top_iff, TopologicalSpace.Opens.ext_iff, Set.ext_iff]

84	/--
85	An `R`-algebra `S` is called standard smooth of relative dimension `n`, if there exists
86	a submersive presentation of dimension `n`.
87	-/
88	class IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension : Prop where
89	  out : ∃ (ι σ : Type) (_ : Finite σ) (_ : Finite ι) (P : SubmersivePresentation R S ι σ),
90	    P.dimension = n

95	    IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S := by
96	  refine ⟨⟨_, _, _, inferInstance,

98	  simp [hP]

11	/-!
12	# Geometrically Irreducible Schemes
13	
14	## Main results
15	- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyIrreducible`:
16	  We say that morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically irreducible if for all `Spec K ⟶ Y` with `K`
17	  a field, `X ×[Y] Spec K` is irreducible.
18	  We also provide the fact that this is stable under base change (by infer_instance)
19	- `GeometricallyIrreducible.iff_geometricallyIrreducible_fiber`:
20	  A scheme is geometrically irreducible over `S` iff the fibers of all
21	  `s : S` are geometrically irreducible.
22	- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace`:
23	  If `X` is geometrically irreducible and universally open (e.g. when flat + finite presentation),
24	  over an irreducible scheme, then `X` is also irreducible.
25	  In particular, the base change of a geometrically irreducible and universally open scheme to an
26	  irreducible scheme is irreducible (by infer_instance).
27	-/
28	
29	universe u
30	
31	@[expose] public section
32	
33	open CategoryTheory MorphismProperty Limits
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	variable {X Y Z S : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ S) (g : Y ⟶ S)
38	
39	/-- We say that morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically irreducible if for all `Spec K ⟶ Y` with `K`
40	a field, `X ×[Y] Spec K` is irreducible. -/
41	@[mk_iff]
42	class GeometricallyIrreducible (f : X ⟶ Y) : Prop where
43	  geometrically_irreducibleSpace : geometrically (IrreducibleSpace ·) f
44	
45	lemma GeometricallyIrreducible.eq_geometrically :
46	    @GeometricallyIrreducible = geometrically (IrreducibleSpace ·) := by
47	  ext; exact geometricallyIrreducible_iff _
48	
49	instance : IsStableUnderBaseChange @GeometricallyIrreducible :=
50	  GeometricallyIrreducible.eq_geometrically ▸ inferInstance
51	
52	instance [GeometricallyIrreducible g] : GeometricallyIrreducible (pullback.fst f g) :=
53	  MorphismProperty.pullback_fst f g inferInstance

74	  wlog H : ∃ x, s = {x} generalizing s

109	    IrreducibleSpace ↥(pullback f g) :=

117	    GeometricallyIrreducible f ↔ ∀ s, GeometricallyIrreducible (f.fiberToSpecResidueField s) := by
118	  simp only [GeometricallyIrreducible.eq_geometrically,

122	    (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) [GeometricallyIrreducible f] [GeometricallyIrreducible g]
123	    [UniversallyOpen f] [UniversallyOpen g] :
124	    GeometricallyIrreducible (f ≫ g) := by
125	  refine ⟨geometrically_iff_of_isClosedUnderIsomorphisms.mpr fun K _ x ↦ ?_⟩
126	  rw [← (pullbackRightPullbackFstIso g x f).hom.homeomorph.irreducibleSpace_iff]

130	/-- If an open subscheme `U` of `X` surjects onto `S` and `X` is geometrically irreducible over `S`,
131	then also `U` is geometrically irreducible over `S`. -/
132	instance {U : Scheme.{u}} {f : U ⟶ X} {g : X ⟶ S} [IsOpenImmersion f] [GeometricallyIrreducible g]
133	    [Surjective (f ≫ g)] :
134	    GeometricallyIrreducible (f ≫ g) := by
135	  rw [GeometricallyIrreducible.iff_geometricallyIrreducible_fiber]
136	  intro s
137	  refine ⟨geometrically_iff_of_isClosedUnderIsomorphisms.mpr fun K _ x ↦ ?_⟩
138	  let i : pullback ((f ≫ g).fiberToSpecResidueField s) x ⟶
139	      pullback (g.fiberToSpecResidueField s) x :=
140	    pullback.map _ _ _ _ (pullback.map _ _ _ _ f (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _) (by simp) (by simp)) (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _)
141	      (by simp [Scheme.Hom.fiberToSpecResidueField]) (by simp)
142	  have : Nonempty ((f ≫ g).fiber s) := by
143	    rw [((f ≫ g).fiberHomeo s).nonempty_congr, Set.nonempty_coe_sort]
144	    exact (Set.singleton_nonempty s).preimage (f ≫ g).surjective
145	  exact i.isOpenEmbedding.irreducibleSpace

7	module
8	
9	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Properties
10	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Fiber
11	
12	/-!
13	# Geometrically-`P` schemes over a field
14	
15	In this file we define the basic interface for properties like geometrically reduced,
16	geometrically irreducible, geometrically connected etc. In this file
17	we treat an abstract property of schemes `P` and derive the general properties that are
18	shared by all of these variants.
19	
20	A morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically `P` if for any field `K` and
21	morphism `Spec K ⟶ Y`, the base change `X ×[Y] Spec K` satisfies `P`.
22	
23	## Main definitions and results
24	
25	- `AlgebraicGeometry.geometrically`: The morphism property of geometrically-`P` morphisms
26	- `AlgebraicGeometry.geometrically_iff_forall_fiberToSpecResidueField`: `f : X ⟶ Y` is
27	  geometrically-`P` if and only if for every `y : Y`, the fiber `f ⁻¹ {y}` is geometrically-`P`
28	  over `Spec κ(y)`.
29	
30	## Notes
31	
32	This contribution was created as part of the Formalising Algebraic Geometry workshop 2025 in
33	Heidelberg.
34	-/
35	
36	@[expose] public section
37	
38	universe u
39	
40	open CategoryTheory Limits CommRingCat
41	
42	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
43	
44	/-- A morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically `P` if for any field `K` and
45	morphism `Spec K ⟶ Y`, the base change `X ×[Y] Spec K` satisfies `P`. -/
46	def geometrically (P : ObjectProperty Scheme.{u}) : MorphismProperty Scheme.{u} :=
47	  fun X Y f ↦ ∀ ⦃K : Type u⦄ [Field K] (y : Spec (.of K) ⟶ Y)
48	    ⦃Z : Scheme.{u}⦄ (fst : Z ⟶ X) (snd : Z ⟶ Spec (.of K)),
49	    IsPullback fst snd f y → P Z
50	
51	lemma geometrically_eq_universally (P : ObjectProperty Scheme.{u}) :
52	    geometrically P = .universally fun X Y _ ↦ IsIntegral Y → Subsingleton Y → P X := by
53	  ext X Y f
54	  refine ⟨fun hf Z W snd q fst h _ _ ↦ ?_, fun hf aK y Z W fst snd h ↦ ?_⟩
55	  · let := (isField_of_isIntegral_of_subsingleton W).toField
56	    apply hf (W.isoSpec.inv ≫ q) snd (fst ≫ W.isoSpec.hom)
57	    apply h.flip.of_iso (.refl _) (.refl _) W.isoSpec (.refl _) <;> simp
58	  · exact hf _ _ _ h.flip inferInstance inferInstance
59	
60	lemma geometrically_inf (P Q : ObjectProperty Scheme.{u}) :
61	    geometrically (P ⊓ Q) = geometrically P ⊓ geometrically Q := by
62	  simp only [geometrically_eq_universally, ← MorphismProperty.universally_inf]
63	  congr with X Y f
64	  exact ⟨fun H ↦ ⟨(H · · |>.1), (H · · |>.2)⟩, fun H a b ↦ ⟨H.1 a b, H.2 a b⟩⟩
65	
66	variable (P : ObjectProperty Scheme.{u})
67	
68	instance : (geometrically P).IsStableUnderBaseChange := by
69	  rw [geometrically_eq_universally]
70	  infer_instance
71	
72	instance [P.IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms] : IsZariskiLocalAtTarget (geometrically P) := by
73	  rw [geometrically_eq_universally]
74	  refine universally_isZariskiLocalAtTarget _ fun {X} Y f ι U hU H _ _ ↦ ?_
75	  obtain ⟨y⟩ := (inferInstance : Nonempty Y)
76	  obtain ⟨i, hy⟩ := hU.exists_mem y
77	  have heq : U i = ⊤ := eq_top_iff.mpr fun z _ ↦ by rwa [Subsingleton.elim z y]
78	  let e : ↑(U i) ≅ Y := Y.isoOfEq heq ≪≫ Y.topIso
79	  let e' : ↑(f ⁻¹ᵁ U i) ≅ X := X.isoOfEq (by simp [heq]) ≪≫ X.topIso
80	  exact P.prop_of_iso e' <| H i (.of_isIso e.inv) (e.hom.homeomorph.subsingleton_congr.mpr ‹_›)
81	
82	section geometrically
83	
84	variable {P : ObjectProperty Scheme.{u}} {X Y : Scheme.{u}} {f : X ⟶ Y}
85	
86	lemma pullback_of_geometrically (hf : geometrically P f) (K : Type u) [Field K]
87	    (y : Spec (.of K) ⟶ Y) : P (Limits.pullback f y) :=
88	  hf _ _ _ (.of_hasPullback _ _)
89	
90	lemma pullback_of_geometrically' (hf : geometrically P f) (K : Type u) [Field K]
91	    (y : Spec (.of K) ⟶ Y) : P (Limits.pullback y f) :=
92	  hf _ _ _ (.flip <| .of_hasPullback _ _)
93	
94	lemma geometrically_iff_of_isClosedUnderIsomorphisms [P.IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms] :
95	    geometrically P f ↔
96	      ∀ (K : Type u) [Field K] (y : Spec (.of K) ⟶ Y), P (Limits.pullback f y) := by
97	  refine ⟨fun h K _ _ ↦ pullback_of_geometrically h _ _, fun H K _ _ Y fst snd h ↦ ?_⟩
98	  exact P.prop_of_iso h.isoPullback.symm (H _ _)
99	
100	lemma fiber_of_geometrically (hf : geometrically P f) (y : Y) : P (f.fiber y) :=
101	  pullback_of_geometrically hf _ _
102	
103	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
104	/-- `P` holds geometrically for `f` if and only if all fibers are geometrically `P`. -/
105	lemma geometrically_iff_forall_fiberToSpecResidueField :
106	    geometrically P f ↔ ∀ (y : Y), geometrically P (f.fiberToSpecResidueField y) := by
107	  refine ⟨fun hf y ↦ (geometrically P).pullback_snd _ _ hf, fun H ↦ ?_⟩
108	  intro K _ y Z fst snd h
109	  obtain ⟨⟨y, φ⟩, rfl⟩ := (Scheme.SpecToEquivOfField _ _).symm.surjective y
110	  let p : Z ⟶ f.fiber y :=
111	    pullback.lift fst (snd ≫ Spec.map φ) (by simp [h.w, Scheme.SpecToEquivOfField])
112	  apply H y (Spec.map φ) p snd
113	  simp only [Scheme.SpecToEquivOfField, Equiv.coe_fn_symm_mk] at h
114	  refine .flip (.of_bot (.flip ?_) ?_ (IsPullback.of_hasPullback f (Y.fromSpecResidueField y)).flip)
115	  · convert h
116	    simp [p]
117	  · simp [p, Scheme.Hom.fiberToSpecResidueField]
118	
119	/-- This holds in particular if `Y = Spec K`. -/
120	lemma self_of_isIntegral_of_geometrically [IsIntegral Y] [Subsingleton Y] (hf : geometrically P f) :
121	    P X := by
122	  rw [geometrically_eq_universally] at hf
123	  exact MorphismProperty.universally_le _ _ hf ‹_› ‹_›
124	
125	variable {P : ObjectProperty Scheme.{u}} {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {f : X ⟶ Spec (.of R)}
126	
127	lemma geometrically_iff_of_commRing :
128	    geometrically P f ↔ ∀ ⦃K : Type u⦄ [Field K] [Algebra R K] ⦃Y : Scheme.{u}⦄ (fst : Y ⟶ X)
129	      (snd : Y ⟶ Spec (.of K)), IsPullback fst snd f (Spec.map <| ofHom (algebraMap R K)) →
130	      P Y := by
131	  refine ⟨fun hs K _ _ Z fst snd h ↦ hs _ _ _ h, fun H K _ y Z fst snd h ↦ ?_⟩
132	  obtain ⟨φ, rfl⟩ := Spec.map_surjective y
133	  algebraize [φ.hom]
134	  exact H fst snd h
135	
136	lemma geometrically_iff_of_commRing_of_isClosedUnderIsomorphisms [P.IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms] :
137	    geometrically P f ↔ ∀ (K : Type u) [Field K] [Algebra R K],
138	      P (Limits.pullback f (Spec.map <| ofHom <| algebraMap R K)) := by
139	  refine ⟨fun hf K _ _ ↦ pullback_of_geometrically hf _ _, fun H ↦ ?_⟩
140	  rw [geometrically_iff_of_isClosedUnderIsomorphisms]
141	  intro K _ y
142	  obtain ⟨φ, rfl⟩ := Spec.map_surjective y
143	  algebraize [φ.hom]
144	  exact H K
145	
146	end geometrically
147	
148	end AlgebraicGeometry

329	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)

338	/-- **`kbar`-algebra preservation of `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso`** (iter-174 Sub-task A

347	lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap
348	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
349	    ((homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar i).toRingHom).comp
350	        (algebraMap kbar

352	            (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))) =
353	      algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by

207	lemma _root_.Algebra.Generators.ker_localizationAway :
208	    (Generators.localizationAway S r).ker = Ideal.span { C r * X () - 1 } := by
209	  have : aeval (S₁ := S) (Generators.localizationAway S r).val =
210	      (mvPolynomialQuotientEquiv S r).toAlgHom.comp
211	        (Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ R (Ideal.span {C r * X () - 1})) := by
212	    ext x

214	      AlgEquiv.toAlgHom_eq_coe, AlgHom.coe_comp, AlgHom.coe_coe, Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ_eq_mk,
215	      Function.comp_apply]
216	    rw [IsLocalization.Away.mvPolynomialQuotientEquiv_apply, aeval_X]
217	  rw [Generators.ker_eq_ker_aeval_val, this, AlgEquiv.toAlgHom_eq_coe, ← RingHom.ker_coe_toRingHom,
218	    AlgHom.comp_toRingHom, ← RingHom.comap_ker]
219	  simp only [AlgEquiv.toAlgHom_toRingHom]
220	  change Ideal.comap _ (RingHom.ker (mvPolynomialQuotientEquiv S r)) = Ideal.span {C r * X () - 1}

228	  toGenerators := Generators.localizationAway S r
229	  relation _ := C r * X () - 1

123	variable (R S) in
124	lemma exists_presentation_fin [FinitePresentation R S] :
125	    ∃ n m, Nonempty (Presentation R S (Fin n) (Fin m)) :=
126	  letI H := FinitePresentation.out (R := R) (A := S)
127	  letI n : ℕ := H.choose
128	  letI f : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R →ₐ[R] S := H.choose_spec.choose
129	  haveI hf : Function.Surjective f := H.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
130	  haveI hf' : (RingHom.ker f).FG := H.choose_spec.choose_spec.2
131	  letI H' := Submodule.fg_iff_exists_fin_generating_family.mp hf'
132	  let m : ℕ := H'.choose
133	  let v : Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin n) R := H'.choose_spec.choose
134	  have hv : Ideal.span (Set.range v) = RingHom.ker f := H'.choose_spec.choose_spec
135	  ⟨n, m,
136	    ⟨{__ := Generators.ofSurjective (fun x ↦ f (.X x)) (by convert hf; ext; simp)
137	      relation := v
138	      span_range_relation_eq_ker := hv.trans (by congr; ext; simp) }⟩⟩

143	def ofFinitePresentationVars [FinitePresentation R S] : ℕ :=

155	def ofFinitePresentation [FinitePresentation R S] :
156	    Presentation R S (Fin (ofFinitePresentationVars R S)) (Fin (ofFinitePresentationRels R S)) :=
157	  (exists_presentation_fin R S).choose_spec.choose_spec.some

176	/-- If `algebraMap R S` is bijective, the empty generators are a presentation with no relations. -/
177	noncomputable def ofBijectiveAlgebraMap (h : Function.Bijective (algebraMap R S)) :
178	    Presentation R S PEmpty.{w + 1} PEmpty.{t + 1} where

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.TensorProduct.RightExactness
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.FinitePresentation
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Extension.Generators
11	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.MvPolynomial.Localization
12	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.MvPolynomial
13	
14	/-!
15	
16	# Presentations of algebras
17	
18	A presentation of an `R`-algebra `S` is a distinguished family of generators and relations.
19	
20	## Main definition
21	
22	- `Algebra.Presentation`: A presentation of an `R`-algebra `S` is a family of
23	  generators with
24	  1. `rels`: The type of relations.
25	  2. `relation : relations → MvPolynomial vars R`: The assignment of
26	     each relation to a polynomial in the generators.
27	- `Algebra.Presentation.IsFinite`: A presentation is called finite if both variables and relations
28	  are finite.
29	- `Algebra.Presentation.dimension`: The dimension of a presentation is the number of generators
30	  minus the number of relations.
31	
32	We also give constructors for localization, base change and composition.
33	
34	## TODO
35	
36	- Define `Hom`s of presentations.
37	
38	## Notes
39	
40	This contribution was created as part of the AIM workshop "Formalizing algebraic geometry"
41	in June 2024.
42	
43	-/
44	
45	@[expose] public section
46	
47	universe t w u v
48	
49	open TensorProduct MvPolynomial
50	
51	variable (R : Type u) (S : Type v) (ι : Type w) (σ : Type t) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
52	
53	/--
54	A presentation of an `R`-algebra `S` is a family of
55	generators with `σ → MvPolynomial ι R`: The assignment of
56	each relation to a polynomial in the generators.
57	-/
58	@[nolint checkUnivs]
59	structure Algebra.Presentation extends Algebra.Generators R S ι where
60	  /-- The assignment of each relation to a polynomial in the generators. -/
61	  relation : σ → toGenerators.Ring
62	  /-- The relations span the kernel of the canonical map. -/
63	  span_range_relation_eq_ker :
64	    Ideal.span (Set.range relation) = toGenerators.ker
65	
66	namespace Algebra.Presentation
67	
68	variable {R S ι σ}
69	variable (P : Presentation R S ι σ)
70	
71	@[simp]
72	lemma aeval_val_relation (i) : aeval P.val (P.relation i) = 0 := by
73	  rw [← RingHom.mem_ker, ← P.ker_eq_ker_aeval_val, ← P.span_range_relation_eq_ker]
74	  exact Ideal.subset_span ⟨i, rfl⟩
75	
76	lemma relation_mem_ker (i) : P.relation i ∈ P.ker := by
77	  rw [← P.span_range_relation_eq_ker]
78	  apply Ideal.subset_span
79	  use i
80	
81	/-- The polynomial algebra w.r.t. a family of generators modulo a family of relations. -/
82	protected abbrev Quotient : Type (max w u) := P.Ring ⧸ P.ker
83	
84	/-- `P.Quotient` is `P.Ring`-isomorphic to `S` and in particular `R`-isomorphic to `S`. -/
85	noncomputable def quotientEquiv : P.Quotient ≃ₐ[P.Ring] S :=
86	  Ideal.quotientKerAlgEquivOfRightInverse (f := Algebra.ofId P.Ring S) (g := P.σ) <| fun x ↦ by
87	    rw [Algebra.ofId_apply, P.algebraMap_apply, P.aeval_val_σ]
88	
89	@[simp]
90	lemma quotientEquiv_mk (p : P.Ring) : P.quotientEquiv p = algebraMap P.Ring S p :=
91	  rfl
92	
93	@[simp]
94	lemma quotientEquiv_symm (x : S) : P.quotientEquiv.symm x = P.σ x :=

import Mathlib
open Algebra MvPolynomial

-- Build SubmersivePresentation R (MvPolynomial Unit R) Unit Empty
noncomputable def polyPres (R : Type) [CommRing R] :
    Algebra.SubmersivePresentation R (MvPolynomial Unit R) Unit Empty :=
  let preGens : Algebra.Generators R (MvPolynomial Unit R) Unit :=
    { val := X
      σ' := id
      aeval_val_σ' := by intro x; simp }
  let pres : Algebra.Presentation R (MvPolynomial Unit R) Unit Empty :=
    { toGenerators := preGens
      relation := Empty.elim
      span_range_relation_eq_ker := by
        simp only [Set.range_eq_empty, Ideal.span_empty]
        rw [eq_comm, Generators.ker_eq_ker_aeval_val]
        rw [← RingHom.injective_iff_ker_eq_bot]
        intro x y hxy
        simpa using hxy }
  let preSub : Algebra.PreSubmersivePresentation R (MvPolynomial Unit R) Unit Empty :=
    { toPresentation := pres
      map := Empty.elim
      map_inj := fun a => a.elim }
  { toPreSubmersivePresentation := preSub
    jacobian_isUnit := by
      show IsUnit preSub.jacobian
      rw [show preSub.jacobian = 1 from ?_]
      · exact isUnit_one
      · -- jacobian is det of empty matrix
        show preSub.jacobiMatrix.det = 1
        exact Matrix.det_isEmpty }

346	forward round-trip then gives `forward (algebraMap kbar Away r) = C r`. -/

349	    ((homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar i).toRingHom).comp

352	            (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))) =
353	      algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by
354	  ext r

358	  -- `HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_eq`), so the composite is exactly `kbarToAwayRingHom`
359	  -- which equals `inverse (C r)` by `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C`.
